ingredients.description: 150 BPM high-energy techno with triple mute groups
Four-layer kick stack low-passed at 1200 Hz for heavy sub weight
NES bass loop with MIDI-controlled sub-octave for massive low-end
Superfork lead with chromatic descent and octave-doubled unison
Dual distortion: bitcrush + squiz on the NES bass for gnarly texture
120 BPM nu-jazz with house kick foundation and jazz sample layers
Jazz piano chords alternating between two voicings on 32-cycle rotation
Warsaw bass with syncopated euclidean-style note triggering pattern
Plucked jazz bass alternating samples every 64 cycles for evolution
MIDI-controlled room reverb and bitcrush on bass for live dynamics
120 BPM Beatober track (Oct 16) - released as La Fin de l'Insouciance

ingredients.code: note ("<[<g4 <g4!3 gs4> gs4 gs4>@3 <c!13 gs4 c c>@2 ...]") # "FMRhodes1"
ingredients.description: Bird-call FM Rhodes melody with chromatic voice-leading (g/gs alternation)

ingredients.code: "weird_dialogs:39" with fix slicing: begin/end for effects/almost/variations/answer
ingredients.description: Vocal dialogue sample surgically sliced into 4 semantic sections for Q&A texture
Atmospheric trance pad layer with sustained legato and reverb wash
MIDI-controlled delay on FMRhodes creating spatial depth on demand
120 BPM nu-jazz with jazz samples and wah-wah guitar
Jazz piano with reversed mirror superimpose creating stereo depth
Warsaw bass with tritone movement (a4 to fs4) and octave doubling
Wah-wah guitar with MIDI sub-octave for funky bass reinforcement

ingredients.code: $ jux rev $ note (...) # "moogBass" # chorus 0.2 # att 0.3 # rel 2
ingredients.description: Moog choir with jux-reversed stereo and slow attack/release for evolving pad
